conditions.description: Progressive intervertebral disc height loss at L1–L2. Reduces spinal canal clearance and contributes to axial back pain and stiffness.
Degenerative changes at L2–L3 with moderate disc height reduction. Increases facet joint loading and may refer pain to the lower back and hips.
Chronic bony anomaly from a vertebral endplate fragment separated during adolescent growth. Stable structurally but can irritate the adjacent disc over time.
Moderate disc height reduction at L4–L5 with possible annular bulging. Common contributor to lower back pain, leg symptoms, and reduced lumbar mobility.
Foraminal stenosis or disc protrusion compressing the L5 nerve root. Can cause radiating pain, numbness, or weakness tracking down the leg into the foot.
Approximately 10% loss of vertebral body height at T9, consistent with early compression or degenerative change. Often asymptomatic but warrants monitoring for progression.
